idea-plugin.name: Spring Transaction Inspector
idea-plugin.description:
Spring Transaction Inspector

Catch Spring @Transactional pitfalls before they reach production! This plugin performs static analysis to detect common transaction anti-patterns and configuration issues.

🎯 Key Features (8 Inspections)

  • AOP Proxy Bypass Detection - Warns when calling @Transactional methods within the same class
  • Invalid Method Modifiers - Detects @Transactional on private/final/static methods that Spring cannot intercept
  • Transaction Propagation Conflicts - Detects MANDATORY/NEVER/REQUIRES_NEW conflicts that cause runtime exceptions or data inconsistency
  • N+1 Query Detection - Identifies lazy-loaded relationships (@OneToMany, @ManyToOne, etc.) accessed in loops and streams
  • ReadOnly Transaction Violations - Detects write operations in @Transactional(readOnly=true) methods
  • Checked Exception Rollback - Warns when checked exceptions won't trigger rollback without proper configuration
  • @Async + @Transactional Conflicts - Detects when transactions won't propagate to async threads
  • ReadOnly Calling Write Methods - Smart detection of propagation conflicts between read-only and write transactions
  • Visual Indicators - Gutter icons show transactional methods at a glance
  • Quick Fixes - One-click solutions for common issues
  • Fully Customizable - Enable/disable specific inspections in Settings

💡 Perfect For

  • Spring Boot developers working with JPA/Hibernate
  • Teams wanting to catch transaction bugs during code review
  • Developers learning Spring transaction management best practices

📖 Learn More

GitHub Repository | Report Issues
idea-plugin.change-notes:
Version 1.1.0 - Quality & Reliability Release (Latest)

  • Jakarta/javax parity: Propagation conflict detection (MANDATORY/NEVER/REQUIRES_NEW) and rollbackOn awareness now work for jakarta.transaction.Transactional and javax.transaction.Transactional, not only Spring.
  • Deeper async detection: Lazy-relationship access in @Async methods is now caught anywhere in a call chain (e.g. user.getOrders().get(0).getItems()), not just first-level getters.
  • OSIV-friendly N+1: New optional setting "Also detect outside @Transactional (OSIV)" for projects relying on Open-EntityManager-In-View.
  • Robust readOnly handling: @Transactional(readOnly = ...) is now resolved via constant evaluation, so Boolean.TRUE, parenthesized expressions and constant references all work.
  • Memory: Replaced an unbounded repository cache with CachedValuesManager tied to PsiModificationTracker, eliminating stale PsiClass retention on long sessions.
  • Quick-fix bug: Remove @Transactional on an @Async/@Transactional pair was deleting @Async instead of @Transactional — fixed.
  • Performance: Each inspection short-circuits at the visitor entry when its toggle is disabled.
  • CI: Tests now run on every PR; release notes are no longer hard-coded in the workflow.

Version 1.0.6 - Build Toolchain Update

  • Build: Upgraded to Gradle 9.4 and IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin 2.12.0 to fix plugin archive extraction issue on IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 (build 261.*)

Version 1.0.5 - IDE Compatibility Update

  • IDE Compatibility: Extended support to IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 (build 261.*)

Version 1.0.4 - Improved Same-Class Call Detection

  • Context-Aware Warnings: Same-class @Transactional method call inspection now differentiates between scenarios:
    • INFO level: When caller has @Transactional and no special propagation - annotation is redundant but joins existing transaction (expected behavior)
    • WARNING level: When called method has special propagation (REQUIRES_NEW, MANDATORY, etc.) - won't work as expected
    • WARNING level: When caller has no @Transactional - annotation will be completely ignored
  • Better Developer Experience: Reduces false positives and helps developers understand when same-class calls are acceptable vs. problematic

Version 1.0.3 - Jakarta EE Support

  • Jakarta Transaction Support: Now detects jakarta.transaction.Transactional and javax.transaction.Transactional
    • AOP Proxy Bypass Detection
    • Invalid Method Modifiers (private/final/static)
    • @Async + @Transactional Conflicts
    • Gutter Icons
    • Transaction Info Action
  • IDE Compatibility: Extended support to IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3 (build 253.*)

Version 1.0.2 - Enhanced Detection

  • New Inspection: Transaction Propagation Conflict Detection
    • MANDATORY: Detects calls without active transaction (prevents IllegalTransactionStateException)
    • NEVER: Detects calls within transactional context (prevents IllegalTransactionStateException)
    • REQUIRES_NEW: Warns about independent transaction creation and data inconsistency risks
  • N+1 Query Detection Improvements:
    • Now detects @ManyToOne(fetch = LAZY) - single entity relationships
    • Now detects @OneToOne(fetch = LAZY) - single entity relationships
    • Accurate detection based on fetch type defaults (LAZY vs EAGER)
  • Total Inspections: 8 comprehensive checks for Spring @Transactional anti-patterns

Version 1.0.1 - Major Improvements

  • Fixed memory leak in AddRollbackForFix
  • Type-based write operation detection (95%+ accuracy)
  • Repository class caching for performance
  • All inspections now have settings toggles

Version 1.0.0 - Initial Release

  • 7 inspections for @Transactional anti-patterns
  • Quick Fixes, Gutter icons, Customizable settings

localInspection.displayName: Write method call from readOnly transaction

action.description: Displays the transaction information of the current method
